live_path.role: CLI entrypoint parses --owner and forwards args.owner into the service call
Service orchestrator that resolves owner provenance, builds the base record, derives the routing key, and emits the payload
Derives effective_owner and owner_source; returns explicit for a non-empty --owner and default otherwise
Persists the base record using the already-resolved effective owner
Builds the stored/base record with name, status, and owner only
Derives routing_key from the already-resolved effective owner and name
Slugifies owner and name and formats owner:name
Emits the final payload by appending owner_source and routing_key to the base record
Copies the base record and appends owner_source and routing_key during emission

owner_source.rule: Returns "explicit" when owner is provided and non-blank; otherwise returns "default" with the configured default owner

routing_key.rule: Formats slugified owner and slugified name as owner:name

test_observations.observation: sync_item("Launch Checklist", "pending", owner="pm-oncall") returns owner_source=explicit and routing_key=pm-oncall:launch-checklist
When --owner is absent, owner_source=default and owner comes from config/defaults.json via _load_defaults
CLI parsing of --owner flows through main(...) into the same emitted payload shape
4 tests passed; behavior and docs references are consistent with the traced path

rejected_decoys.reason: Unused helper; no live caller in the repo. It would infer owner_source from a record, but the live path passes owner_source directly into serialize_payload.
Legacy helper is not called by sync_item or cli main. The live path uses make_record instead.
Legacy helper is not called by sync_item. The live path uses sync_app.serializer::build_routing_key.
Narrative hypothesis, not executable behavior. Its claims conflict with the code path and tests.
